'''Timothy Aiden Smith''' (born 13 January 1985) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer. He previously played for Super League clubs [[Wigan Warriors]] and [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] (twice), and [[Salford Red Devils]], and in Australia for the [[Parramatta Eels]] and the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] of the [[National Rugby League]] in the NRL. Smith's usual position was in the halves.

==Playing career==
===Parramatta Eels===
[[File:Tim Smith (23 April 2005).jpg|thumb|right|Smith in 2005]]
Smith started his NRL career with the [[Parramatta Eels]] and received much acclaim from Parramatta legend [[Peter Sterling (rugby league commentator)|Peter Sterling]], who "handed over" the Eels number 7 jersey in an episode of ''[[The NRL Footy Show]]'' in the middle of 2005.  Smith played 26 games in the [[2005 NRL season]] as Parramatta won the [[Minor Premiership]].  Along with [[St. George Illawarra]], Parramatta were favorites to win the premiership in 2005 but were defeated 29–0 in the preliminary final by [[North Queensland Cowboys|North Queensland]] at [[Telstra Stadium]] in an upset loss.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/news/league/cowboys-stun-eels-to-make-grand-final/2005/09/25/1127586741021.html/|title=North Queensland stun Parramatta to make grand final|website=www.smh.com.au|date=25 September 2005}}</ref>

After leading the competition in try assists he won the rookie of the year award at the [[2005 Dally M Awards]]. With 40 try assists in his début season Smith was named the [[2005 Parramatta Eels season]]'s rookie of the year. As of the [[2019 NRL season]], Smith's 40 try assists is still an [[NRL]] record.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/parramatta-eels-five-times-they-should-have-been-set-for-a-big-season-but-flopped/news-story/70bdcb6a002101a0640921d067eb4c49/|title=Parramatta Eels: Five times they should have been set for a big season but flopped|website=Fox Sports|date=20 March 2018}}</ref>

In December 2005, Smith was allegedly involved in a street scuffle with a man in the inner-Sydney suburb of [[The Rocks, New South Wales|the Rocks]].<ref>{{cite news| url=http://www.theadvertiser.news.com.au/common/story_page/0,5936,17829027%255E23214,00.html| title=AdelaideNow... Fan claims Eels star attacked him| website=Theadvertiser.news.com.au| access-date=15 October 2017}}{{Dead link|date=November 2019 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref> He denied any such action and no charges were laid.{{Citation needed|date=December 2007}}
In 2006, Smith was involved in an altercation with Australian cricketer [[Michael Clarke (cricketer)|Michael Clarke]] after Clarke confronted Smith at the Northies Hotel in [[Cronulla]]. It was alleged that Smith was talking to Clarke's ex-girlfriend which led to the altercation.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/what-on-earth-is-going-on-in-the-head-of-tim-smith-20060624-gdntoa.html|title = What on earth is going on in the head of Tim Smith?|date = 24 June 2006}}</ref>

In the [[2007 NRL season]], Smith played 27 games for Parramatta as the club made it to the preliminary final but fell short of a grand final appearance losing to Melbourne 26–10. In December 2007, further allegations of public drunken behaviour were made against Smith.<ref>{{cite news|publisher=Fox Sports News (Australia)|title=Eels halfback could face sack|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,22878518-23214,00.html|access-date=5 December 2007|date=6 December 2007}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/melbourne-v-parramatta-20091002-gdtqv2.html/|title=Melbourne v Parramatta|website=SMH|date=2 October 2009}}</ref>

Smith was suspended indefinitely by Parramatta. He remained on contract with the club, and Parramatta stated the most important issue was his health. Smith booked himself into full-time rehabilitation interstate.<ref name="Eels' Smith suspended indefinitely">{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,22923430-23214,00.html|title=Eels' Smith suspended indefinitely|publisher=Fox|date=14 December 2007|access-date=14 December 2007}}</ref> On 8 January 2008 the Parramatta Club reviewed his behaviour at the rehabilitation clinic and lifted his suspension but he was subject to a number of sanctions which included a fine and charity work.<ref>{{cite news|publisher=Abc News|title=Eels lift Smith's suspension|url=http://www.abc.net.au/news/stories/2008/01/08/2134235.htm|archive-url=https://archive.today/20120717110919/http://www.abc.net.au/news/stories/2008/01/08/2134235.htm|url-status=dead|archive-date=17 July 2012|access-date=8 January 2008|date=8 January 2008}}</ref>

Early in the 2008 season, after just three games of football, Smith advised his club and teammates that he was suffering from bipolar disorder.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.parramattasun.com.au/news/local/news/general/eels-tim-smith-reveals-bipolar-disorder/468594.aspx |title=Eels' Tim Smith reveals bipolar disorder - Local News - News - General - Parramatta Sun |access-date=5 December 2012 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://archive.today/20120712085304/http://www.parramattasun.com.au/news/local/news/general/eels-tim-smith-reveals-bipolar-disorder/468594.aspx |archive-date=12 July 2012  }}</ref> He then gave a statement to the media saying he had been given an indefinite leave from football, saying he was having trouble coping with the constant criticism from the media.

Smith returned to his family in the Gold Coast, with the aim to rest and recuperate with the aim of returning to professional rugby league in the future.<ref name="Tim Smith talks to Peter Sterling">{{cite web|url=http://video.msn.com/video.aspx/?mkt=en-au&brand=ninemsn&tab=m167&mediaid=194625&from=39&vid=AB44D2A1-1D30-4742-989E-82B1A13CBDEB&playlist=videoByTag:mk:en-AU:vs:0:tag:ausport_ausportsgeneral:ns:MSNVideo_Top_Cat:ps:10:sd:-1:ind:1:ff:8A&wa=wsignin1.0|title=Tim Smith talks to Peter Sterling|publisher=NineMSN|date=5 June 2008|access-date=5 June 2008}}{{Dead link|date=November 2019 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref>

=== Wigan Warriors ===
[[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] announced in June 2008 that they had signed Smith for the remainder of the 2008 season and 2009.{{citation needed|date=June 2025}}

Smith linked up with his new teammates in July once his work-permit has been approved, after weeks of visa complications.<ref name="Smith set for Wigan debut">{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,24004127-23214,00.html|title=Smith set for Wigan debut|work=Fox Sports|date=11 July 2008|access-date=11 July 2008}}</ref> He played his professional début for the club in a home loss to [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]], although the mental illness that had ended his season for the Parramatta Eels and lack of match fitness did produce some concern about his ability to play rugby league in Europe's top-flight competition, which was reflected in the terms of his contract to Wigan, despite his claims that his bipolar condition was now under control:

{{quote box
| quote=We acknowledge some risks – reflected in the contract – in signing Tim after his early-season illness difficulties but Tim now feels ready to re-launch his Rugby League career away from the Sydney media pressure. We believe the supportive and professional environment at the Wigan club is ideal to help him achieve this.
| source=[[Ian Lenagan]]<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.metro.co.uk/sport/rugby/article.html?in_article_id=168054&in_page_id=45&in_a_source=|title=Controversial Smith joins Wigan|date=9 June 2008|publisher=metro.co.uk|access-date=17 April 2009}}</ref>
| align=center
| width=85%}}

=== Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks ===
He signed with the [[Brisbane Broncos]] for the [[2010 NRL season]] but did not make an appearance, which led to his move to the Sharks. In late 2009, Smith began training with the [[Brisbane Broncos]] in a bid to win a return to the NRL through their [[Queensland Cup]] feeder side [[Easts Tigers]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/smith-return-gives-broncos-options/story-e6frexnr-1225808337308|title=Smith gives Broncos options|website=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=15 October 2017}}</ref> Months later, he signed with the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]]. In his first match back in the NRL, he played his former club the [[Parramatta Eels]], and was influential in leading the Sharks to an 11–0 win, scoring a penalty goal and a field goal.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,26915131-5018866,00.html?from=public_rss|title=FOX SPORTS - Live Sports Scores - NRL, AFL, Cricket Scores|website=FOX SPORTS|access-date=15 October 2017}}</ref>
However Smith's form subsequently dropped, which resulted in his axing to the [[NSW Cup]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://sportal.com.au/league-news-display/smith-gets-the-chop-94818 |title=Smith gets the chop - League - Sportal Australia |website=sportal.com.au |access-date=13 January 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100715132338/http://sportal.com.au/league-news-display/smith-gets-the-chop-94818 |archive-date=15 July 2010 |url-status=dead}}</ref>

===Wakefield Trinity===
Smith signed with [[Wakefield Trinity]] for the 2012 season and made his first public appearance at the Trinity kit launch on 8 December.

===Salford Red Devils===
Smith signed with Salford Red Devils towards the end of the 2013 season, with the chairman of his former club releasing a statement confirming that Salford had paid 'a substantial fee' for the half back.{{citation needed|date=June 2024}}

On 10 June, Smith returned to Wakefield on loan until the end of the season. However, on the same day, Salford owner Marwan Koukash had to ditch players to fit [[Kevin Locke (rugby league)|Kevin Locke]] in the team. Locke had a high salary, and Tim and teammate [[Shannan McPherson]] had to leave in order for Locke to fit under the Salary Cap and avoid breach.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/article/30421/locke-arrival-forces-salford-exits|title=RUGBY-LEAGUE.COM|website=Rugby-League.com|access-date=15 October 2017}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12040/9343914/super-league-wakefield-wildcats-land-salford-red-devils-tim-smith-on-loan|title=Super League: Tim Smith and Shannon McPherson leave Salford Red Devils|website=1.skysports.com|access-date=15 October 2017}}</ref>

===Return to Wakefield Trinity===
In October 2014, Smith rejoined Wakefield on a one-year deal.{{citation needed|date=June 2025}} On 1 October 2015, Smith was terminated by Wakefield for gross misconduct and for bringing the club into disrepute.<ref name="bbc">{{cite web | url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/34411613 | title=Tim Smith: Wakefield Trinity Wildcats sack scrum-half | publisher=[[BBC]] | date=1 October 2015 | accessdate=6 June 2025}}</ref>

It was alleged at the time that Smith along with teammate [[Kevin Locke (rugby league)|Kevin Locke]] had crashed his car into another vehicle and into the wall of a house. Smith attended Beverley magistrates court and was banned from driving for 28 months and fined £900 after admitting failing to provide a specimen following the car crash in September 2015. Smith was also handed a £600 fine for careless driving.<ref name="bbc"/>

Wakefield chairman Michael Carter said to the media "A letter was last night sent to Tim Smith and his lawyer saying we were dismissing him for gross misconduct and bringing the club into disrepute. After Smith was terminated by Wakefield, [[Kevin Locke (rugby league)|Kevin Locke]] was also terminated on the same day.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/34411613|title = Wakefield sack scrum-half Smith|work = BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.sbs.com.au/news/wakefield-sack-tim-smith-after-driving-ban|title = Wakefield sack Tim Smith after driving ban | work=SBS News }}</ref><ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/29575016|title = Wakefield re-sign half-back Smith|work = BBC Sport}}</ref>

==Post-playing career==
In November 2018, Smith was announced as assistant coach of the Victoria Thunderbolts.  On 19 November 2018, Smith played for Parramatta in the Legends of League tournament held at the [[Central Coast Stadium]] in Gosford. Smith became coach of the Quaker Valley rugby team in 2019.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.parraeels.com.au/news/2018/11/19/parramatta-at-legends-of-league-tournament/|title = Parramatta at Legends of League tournament|date = 18 November 2018}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2018/11/05/thunderbolts-make-2019-coaching-appointments/|title = Thunderbolts Make 2019 Coaching Appointments|date = 5 November 2018}}</ref>
